fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New York Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to New York - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity new york, how to transfer dietitian license to new york, dietitian compact new york, new york dietitian endorsement application

board.name: New York State Board for Dietetics and Nutrition
board.mailing_address:
New York State Education Department
Office of the Professions
Dietetics and Nutrition Unit
89 Washington Avenue
Albany, NY 12234-1000

license_types.title: Certified Dietitian-Nutritionist (CDN)
license_types.description: The standard credential for practicing dietetics and nutrition in New York State. Demands completion of a Master's degree, ACEND-approved supervised practice, and passing the national CDR exam.

steps.title: Meet Education, Experience, and Examination Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you have completed an approved bachelor's or master's degree program, 800 hours of supervised experience, and passed the national examination administered by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

steps.title: Create NY.gov Account and Link Records
steps.description: If you don't have one, create a NY.gov account. Then, link your existing NYSED Office of the Professions records to your NY.gov account via the NYSED Online Applications portal to prepare for your application.

steps.title: Submit Application and Fee Online
steps.description: Log in to the NYSED Online Applications system, complete the 'Application for Licensure' (Form 1) for Dietitians and Nutritionists, and pay the $292 licensure fee.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts (Form 2), verification of supervised experience (Form 4), and your national examination results (CDR sends directly) to be sent directly to the New York State Education Department by the issuing institutions or organizations.

documents.detail: Must be sent directly from your degree-granting university to the NYSED.
Proof of active registration sent directly from the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
Certificate of completion for the mandatory NYS child abuse identification coursework.

provisional_text: New York State does not issue temporary permits or provisional licenses for dietitians or nutritionists.
reciprocity_text: New York does not maintain automatic reciprocity agreements with other states. Out-of-state applicants must apply for a new license by equivalence, providing direct verification of their out-of-state license, CDR registration, and proof they meet all New York education and experience standards.

faqs.question: How much does a New York dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial cost is $294, which covers the NYSED's $115 application fee and the $179 fee for your first three-year registration period.

faqs.question: Do I need a master's degree to become a CDN in New York?
faqs.answer: Yes. As of January 1, 2024, a minimum of a master's degree is required by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) to sit for the national RDN exam, which serves as the exam basis for NY state certification.

faqs.question: Is continuing education required to renew a New York CDN license?
faqs.answer: The NYSED does not mandate additional state-level continuing education for renewal, but you must maintain your active CDR registration, which requires completing 75 hours of continuing professional education every 5 years.

faqs.question: Does New York have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in New York. To work in New York, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to New York?
Can I start working in New York while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: New York does not maintain automatic reciprocity agreements with other states. Out-of-state applicants must apply for a new license by equivalence, providing direct verification of their out-of-state license, CDR registration, and proof they meet all New York education and experience standards.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in New York
hero.dek: To apply for a Dietitian & Nutritionist license in New York, applicants must use the NYSED Online Applications system after meeting all education, experience, and examination requirements.

official_links.registration_steps: 1. Create a NY.gov account if you do not already have one. 2. Link your existing NYSED Office of the Professions records (if any) to your NY.gov account. 3. Log in to the NYSED Online Applications system to access and submit your application.
